diary: Monday started early with decent coffee at home before the drive out to Redmond. The light rail was down, which honestly didn't affect me much since I drive to the turbine sites anyway. Spent most of the day up on the towers doing maintenance checks—beautiful weather for it, clear skies and that perfect early-June warmth. Grabbed lunch at Sammamish Valley Café, nothing special but it hit the spot after being out in the sun. Heard some folks at the café talking about the USA-Wales match happening downtown, but I was already committed to finishing up the job. On the way back through Capitol Hill I stopped by Elliott Bay Books for a bit—needed to decompress after the long day. Found a used copy of something I'd been looking for. Then straight to pottery class in Ballard, which was exactly what I needed. There's something about working with clay after working with metal and machinery all day that just centers me.
thought_process: I knew the light rail maintenance might complicate things, but my job site route doesn't depend on it so I didn't stress. The weather was perfect for turbine work—no wind issues, good visibility—so I stayed focused and pushed through the afternoon. I caught wind of the World Cup match but honestly I wasn't tempted to duck out early for a bar; I'm not that invested in soccer. What I *was* craving was some mental space, so stopping at Elliott Bay felt right. By evening I needed something tactile and creative to balance out the technical problem-solving of the job. Pottery's become my reset button, and I scheduled it deliberately for tonight knowing I'd want it.
world_event_prompt: Sunny Monday in early June, high of 74F. FIFA World Cup group stage match (USA vs Wales) airs at 11am PDT — bars downtown open early. Light Rail closed between Westlake and University District 9am-3pm for maintenance.
